LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMMISSION'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. THE COASTAL COMMISSION HAS TWENTY DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE WRITTEN ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER THAT SPECIFICALLY RESPONDS TO THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE VERIFIED FIRST AMENDED PETITION. A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE MAY BE HELD ON MAY 8, 9 OR 10 BY CONTACTING DEPT. 206. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: LAURA A. REDING, CSR 	711 =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION 302, INTERVENOR EVARISTE GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IN ACTION. PROPOSED INTERVENOR EVARISTE GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO INTERVENE IS GRANTED. EVARISTE'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF EDMOND GHANDOUR ARE OVERRULED. THE PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. EVARISTE SHALL FILE THE COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: STEPHEN MURPHY, CLERK: M. OTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2017 Continued To Aug-18-17 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 306

Evariste Group, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene In Action
Off Calendar Jun-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: INTERVENOR EVARISTE GROUP, LLC'S Motion For Leave To Intervene In Action. Proposed Intervenor Evariste Group, LLC's motion to intervene is granted. Assuming arguendo that mandatory intervention is not warranted, the court finds that this is an appropriate case for permissive intervention. Permissive intervention is proper where "(1) the proposed intervenor has a direct interest in the matter, (2) intervention will not enlarge the issues in the litigation, and (3) the reasons for the intervention outweigh any opposition by the present parties." (Lindelli v. Town of San Anselmo (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1499, 1504.) In this case, Evariste has a direct interest in the matter as a member of the entity that allegedly holds title and as SNG's lender, Evariste's intervention will not enlarge the issues in this writ action, and Evariste's interest in monitoring and participating this litigation and protecting its interests outweigh SNG's opposition. Evariste's objections to the declaration of Edmond Ghandour are overruled. The parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Evariste shall file the complaint in intervention within 20 days of the notice of entry of order. The court received SNG's surreply and Evariste's response to surreply. Since they were filed without court approval, the court declines to review them. Order is signed. Judge: Stephen Murphy, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported =(302/SM)

Petitioner Security National Guaranty'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment (Ccp 1094)
Off Calendar Apr-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN H. KAUFMANN OF THE NOSSAMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-213-613-7875 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO OPPOSING PARTY TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Security National Guaranty, Inc.'s motion for judgment is denied without prejudice. Respondent California Coastal Commission's request for leave to amend is granted. The Coastal Commission has twenty days from notice of entry of the written order to file an amended answer that specifically responds to the allegations in the verified first amended petition. A settlement conference may be held on May 8, 9 or 10 by contacting Dept. 206. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711 =302/ajr
